channel.title: AccessiTech Blog
channel.description: Explore expert-written articles, tools, and tips to help you build better, more inclusive digital products.

item.title: AI Governance Is an Architecture Problem — Not a Compliance Checkbox
item.description: AI governance requires accountability architecture, not compliance checklists. When 'the AI did it' becomes the legal defense, that's an architecture failure.
item.excerpt: AI governance requires accountability architecture, not compliance checklists. When 'the AI did it' becomes the legal defense, that's an architecture failure.
item.media:description: AI Governance Is an Architecture Problem — Not a Compliance Checkbox

item.title: AI for Coding Accessibility
item.description: How developers with disabilities use AI like Copilot to code through chronic pain—where it excels, where it fails, and why human judgment still matters.
item.excerpt: How developers with disabilities use AI like Copilot to code through chronic pain—where it excels, where it fails, and why human judgment still matters.
item.media:description: Yellow text on dark blue background saying, AI for Coding Accessibility, How Tools Like Copilot Help Me Ship Code Despite Chronic Pain

item.title: Building a More Accessible Future Through Design and Technology
item.description: How lived experience with Psoriatic Arthritis drives AccessiTech’s mission—audits, mentorship, and open-source tools bridging accessibility theory and practice.
item.excerpt: How lived experience with Psoriatic Arthritis drives AccessiTech’s mission—audits, mentorship, and open-source tools bridging accessibility theory and practice.
item.media:description: Dark blue text on yellow background saying, Building a More Accessible Future Through Design and Technology

item.title: How AccessiTech Meets You Where You Are
item.description: Most 'accessible' tech still fails disabled users. AccessiTech's audits, QA, and mentorship go beyond compliance to rebuild genuine inclusion.
item.excerpt: Most 'accessible' tech still fails disabled users. AccessiTech's audits, QA, and mentorship go beyond compliance to rebuild genuine inclusion.
item.media:description: Black text on pink background saying "Accessibility Isn't One-Size-Fits-All How AccessiTech Meets You Where You Are"

item.title: Introduction to WCAG - What It Is and Why It Matters
item.description: Learn what WCAG is, why web accessibility matters, and how it impacts users, laws, and inclusive design practices. Start building accessible websites today.
item.excerpt: Learn what WCAG is, why web accessibility matters, and how it impacts users, laws, and inclusive design practices. Start building accessible websites today.
item.media:description: Dark blue text on yellow background saying, Introduction to WCAG, What it is and Why it Matters

item.title: Memory Lock-In
item.description: When AI governance lives inside proprietary harnesses, acquisitions can shift liability retroactively. Memory lock-in is vendor lock-in's new form.
item.excerpt: When AI governance lives inside proprietary harnesses, acquisitions can shift liability retroactively. Memory lock-in is vendor lock-in's new form.
item.media:description: Yellow text on gradient background saying, AccessiTech

item.title: The Four Principles of Accessibility (POUR)
item.description: A practical guide to POUR—Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Robust—the four WCAG principles for accessible web experiences, with examples.
item.excerpt: A practical guide to POUR—Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Robust—the four WCAG principles for accessible web experiences, with examples.
item.media:description: Four colored blocks labeled Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Robust

item.title: When 'The AI Did It' Is No Longer a Defense
item.description: When AI systems cause harm, someone made the architecture decisions that created the accountability gap. Legal teams need to understand three specific failure modes before their next AI procurement.
item.excerpt: When AI systems cause harm, someone made the architecture decisions that created the accountability gap. Legal teams need to understand three specific failure modes before their next AI procurement.
item.media:description: When 'The AI Did It' Is No Longer a Defense

channel.title: WCAG Explained
channel.description: Learn about the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) and how to implement them effectively.

item.title: 1.1.1 - Non-text Content
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.1.1 (Non-text Content)—what it means, why it matters, and how to provide accessible alternatives for images, icons…
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures accessible alternatives for images, icons, and other non-text content.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.1.1 Explained, Non-text Content"

item.title: 1.2.1 - Audio-only and Video-only (Prerecorded)
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.2.1 (Audio-only and Video-only, Prerecorded)—what it means, why it matters, and how to make media content accessible.
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures audio-only and video-only content is accessible with captions or transcripts.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.2.1 Explained, Audio-only and Video-only (Prerecorded)"

item.title: 1.2.2 - Captions (Prerecorded)
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.2.2 (Captions, Prerecorded)—what it means, why it matters, and how to make video content accessible with captions.
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures prerecorded video content is accessible with captions.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.2.2 Explained, Captions (Prerecorded)"

item.title: 1.2.3 - Audio Description or Media Alternative (Prerecorded)
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.2.3 (Audio Description or Media Alternative, Prerecorded)—what it means, why it matters, and how to make video content…
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures video content is accessible for users who are blind or have low vision with audio descriptions or media alternatives.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.2.3 Explained, Audio Description or Media Alternative (Prerecorded)"

item.title: 1.2.4 - Captions (Live)
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.2.4 (Captions, Live)—what it means, why it matters, and how to make live video content accessible with captions.
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures live video content is accessible with captions.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.2.4 Explained, Captions (Live)"

item.title: 1.2.5 - Audio Description (Prerecorded)
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.2.5 (Audio Description, Prerecorded)—what it means, why it matters, and how to make prerecorded video content…
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures prerecorded video content is accessible with audio descriptions.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.2.5 Explained, Audio Description (Prerecorded)"

item.title: 1.2.6 - Sign Language (Prerecorded)
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.2.6 (Sign Language, Prerecorded)—what it means, why it matters, and how to make video content accessible with sign…
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures prerecorded video content is accessible with sign language interpretation.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.2.6 Explained, Sign Language (Prerecorded)"

item.title: 1.2.7 - Extended Audio Description (Prerecorded)
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.2.7 (Extended Audio Description, Prerecorded)—what it means, why it matters, and how to make video content accessible…
item.excerpt: Requires extended audio descriptions for prerecorded video, aiding users who are blind or have low vision.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.2.7 Explained, Extended Audio Description (Prerecorded), Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.2.7 Explained"

item.title: 1.2.8 - Media Alternative (Prerecorded)
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.2.8 (Media Alternative, Prerecorded)—what it means, why it matters, and how to provide a full text alternative for…
item.excerpt: Requires a full text alternative for synchronized media, ensuring accessibility for users who cannot access audio or video content.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.2.8 Explained, Media Alternative (Prerecorded)"

item.title: 1.2.9 - Audio-only (Live)
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.2.9 (Audio-only, Live)—what it means, why it matters, and how to make live audio content accessible.
item.excerpt: Ensures live audio content is accessible, for example by providing live captions.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.2.9 Explained, Audio Only (Live)"

item.title: 1.3.1 - Info and Relationships
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure information and relationships are…
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures information and relationships are programmatically determined or available in text.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.3.1 Explained, Info and Relationships"

item.title: 1.3.2 - Meaningful Sequence
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.3.2 (Meaningful Sequence)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure content is presented in a logical order for…
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures content is presented in a logical order for all users.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.3.2 Explained, Meaningful Sequence"

item.title: 1.3.3 - Sensory Characteristics
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.3.3 (Sensory Characteristics)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure instructions don’t rely on shape…
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures instructions do not rely on shape, color, or sound alone.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.3.3 Explained, Sensory Characteristics"

item.title: 1.3.4 - Orientation
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.3.4 (Orientation)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure content is not restricted to a single display…
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures content is not restricted to a single display orientation.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.3.4 Explained, Orientation"

item.title: 1.3.5 - Identify Input Purpose
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose)—what it means, why it matters, and how to help browsers and assistive tech identify the…
item.excerpt: Helps browsers and assistive technologies identify the purpose of input fields, improving usability and accessibility.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.3.5 Explained, Identify Input Purpose"

item.title: 1.3.6 - Identify Purpose
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.3.6 (Identify Purpose)—what it means, why it matters, and how to help browsers and assistive tech identify the purpose…
item.excerpt: Helps browsers and assistive technologies identify the purpose of user interface components, enhancing accessibility.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.3.6 Explained, Identify Input Purpose"

item.title: 1.4.1 - Use of Color
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.4.1 (Use of Color)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure information is not conveyed by color alone.
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures information is not conveyed by color alone.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.4.1 Explained, Use of Color"

item.title: 1.4.10 - Reflow
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.4.10 (Reflow)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure your content works on any screen size.
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures content works on any screen size or orientation.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.4.10 Explained, Reflow"

item.title: 1.4.11 - Non-text Contrast
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.4.11 (Non-text Contrast)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure all visual elements are distinguishable.
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures user interface components and graphical objects have sufficient contrast for visibility.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast"

item.title: 1.4.12 - Text Spacing
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.4.12 (Text Spacing)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure your content is readable with custom spacing.
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures content remains readable when users adjust text spacing for better readability.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.4.12, Text Spacing"

item.title: 1.4.13 - Content on Hover or Focus
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.4.13 (Content on Hover or Focus)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure popups and tooltips are accessible.
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures tooltips and popups are accessible, dismissible, and do not interfere with content.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.4.13, Content on Hover or Focus"

item.title: 1.4.2 - Audio Control
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.4.2 (Audio Control)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure users can control audio that plays automatically.
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures users can control audio that plays automatically.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.4.2 Explained, Audio Control"

item.title: 1.4.3 - Contrast (Minimum)
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.4.3 (Contrast Minimum)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure text is readable for everyone.
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures text is readable against its background for all users.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.4.3 Explained, Contrast (Minimum)"

item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.4.4 (Resize Text)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure text remains readable when users zoom or increase…
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures text remains readable when users zoom or increase font size.

item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.4.5 (Images of Text)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure text is real, not just an image.
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures text is real and not just an image for accessibility.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.4.5 Explained, Images of Text"

item.title: 1.4.6 - Contrast (Enhanced)
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.4.6 (Contrast Enhanced)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure text is readable for users with low vision.
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures text is readable for users with low vision by enhancing contrast.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 1.4.6 Explained, Contrast (Enhanced)"

item.excerpt: This guideline ensures speech audio is clear and not obscured by background sounds.

item.excerpt: This guideline ensures users have control over how text is visually presented.

item.title: 1.4.9 - Images of Text (No Exception)
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 1.4.9 (Images of Text No Exception)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure all text is real, not just an image.
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures all text is real and not just an image.

item.title: 2.1.1 - Keyboard Accessible
item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 2.1.1 (Keyboard Accessible)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure all functionality is available from a keyboard.
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures all functionality is accessible using only a keyboard.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 2.1.1 Explained, Keyboard Accessible"

item.excerpt: This guideline ensures keyboard users can navigate to and away from any component without getting trapped.

item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 2.1.3 (Keyboard, No Exception)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure all functionality is accessible via…
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures all functionality is accessible via keyboard with absolutely no exceptions.

item.excerpt: This guideline ensures keyboard shortcuts can be turned off, remapped, or only activated when a specific element has focus.

item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 2.2.1 (Timing Adjustable)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure users can extend or turn off time limits.
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures users can extend or turn off time limits for accessibility.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 2.2.1 Explained, Timing Adjustable"

item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure users can control moving, blinking, or…
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures users can control moving, blinking, or auto-updating content.
item.media:description: Blue text on yellow background saying, "Web Content Accessibiilty Guiedlines (WCAG) 2.2.2 Explained, Pause, Stop, Hide"

item.description: A practical guide to WCAG Guideline 2.2.3 (No Timing)—what it means, why it matters, and how to ensure content is accessible without time limits.
item.excerpt: This guideline ensures content is accessible without unnecessary time limits.

item.excerpt: This guideline ensures users can skip repetitive content to access main content quickly.

item.title: AI Governance Is an Architecture Problem — Not a Compliance Checkbox
item.description: AI governance requires accountability architecture, not compliance checklists. When 'the AI did it' becomes the legal defense, that's an architecture failure.
